# SkillGuard

> Permission lockfile and security scanner for Agent Skills.

SkillGuard helps AI agents and developers inspect Agent Skills before install or execution. It statically scans `SKILL.md`, scripts, dependency hints, and configuration files, then reports concrete evidence, inferred capabilities, and permission diffs against a lockfile.

## Quick Start

- Scan one Skill: `python -m skillguard scan <skill-dir> --format json`
- Scan a collection: `python -m skillguard scan-all <skills-root> --format json`
- Lock trusted permissions: `python -m skillguard lock <skill-dir>`
- Diff permission drift: `python -m skillguard diff <skill-dir> --format json`
- Verify permission drift: `python -m skillguard verify <skill-dir> --format json`
- Human-readable output: omit `--format json`

## Agent Integration

- One-prompt install: `Please install SkillGuard: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tom1997/SkillGuard/main/docs/install.md`
- Agent usage guide: `docs/agent-usage.md`
- Chinese agent usage guide: `docs/agent-usage.zh-CN.md`
- Installable Skill wrapper: `skills/skillguard-auditor/SKILL.md`
- Rule authoring guide: `docs/rules.md`
- Real-world smoke tests: `docs/real-world-smoke-tests.md`

## Core Concepts

- Findings include rule ID, severity, category, file, line number, snippet, message, remediation, and capabilities.
- Capabilities summarize filesystem, network, command, environment, and privilege needs.
- Dataflow signals summarize possible user input, local data, and outbound network write evidence.
- High and critical findings should require explicit user review.
- The current scanner is static and does not execute target Skill code.

## Important Commands

```bash
python -m pip install -e ".[dev]"
python -m pytest
python -m skillguard scan examples/exfiltration-skill
python -m skillguard scan-all examples --format json
```

## Roadmap

- GitHub URL scanning
- SARIF output
- GitHub Action
- ignore comments
- Docker sandbox
- lock and verify workflow
